title: Cross-form and cross-event references use four canonical XPath shapes
description: Cross-form / cross-event field references use one of four exact XPath shapes: (1) same-event-same-form direct reference, (2) different-event lookup by event OID, (3) current-event-OID lookup, (4) pulldata from <study_id>_tpt. All cross-form references require bind__oc_external='clinicaldata' (or the CSV name for pulldata). Referenced event OIDs go into settings.crossform_references as a comma-separated list.
rationale: OC4's expression evaluator only resolves cross-form references via these four canonical patterns. Any other shape (custom XPath, missing bind::oc:external marker, unregistered event OID) silently returns empty at runtime — the field appears blank to data entry without raising an error.

flag.message: Field ${field.name} has cross-form dependency; verify calculation matches one of the four canonical XPath shapes (same-event-same-form / different-event-by-OID / current-event-OID / pulldata <study_id>_tpt) and bind__oc_external is set to 'clinicaldata' or the pulldata CSV name.

effect.soft: Inspect field.calculation against the four canonical XPath shapes. If the shape is unfamiliar, prefer pulldata from <study_id>_tpt or document the reasoning. Add referenced event OIDs to settings.crossform_references.

history.note: Imported from prompts.py RULE OC-4 during Phase B.1a. Pattern-matching the four XPath shapes from the calculation string is a regex-shaped check; deferred to Phase B/C DSL extension. require + flag covers the mechanical bind__oc_external presence today; soft directive carries the pattern-match guidance to Claude. Kind revised from `structured` (per B.0 inventory) to `hybrid` during B.1a translation because the four-canonical-XPath-shapes pattern-match requires AI judgment until DSL extension lands; soft directive carries the guidance.
